---
title: "Engineering Systems Roadmap"
description: "A dependency-aware route through dynamics, signals, control, computation, and stochastic decision-making for readers who want a systems-facing math path."
page-type: roadmap
status: published
update-status: current
level: bridge
topic: engineering-systems
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/single-variable-calculus/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/multivariable-calculus/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose a sensible math-to-systems study order."
  - "Recognize where engineering-systems study branches into control, sensing, communication, and computation."
  - "See which parts of the current site already support systems-facing reading routes."
applications:
  - engineering-systems
  - control
  - communication
  - scientific-computing
keywords:
  - roadmap
  - engineering systems
  - control
  - signal processing
  - scientific computing
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-25
source-check-date: 2026-04-25
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
---

## Purpose

This roadmap is for readers who want math to feel connected to systems that evolve, sense, estimate, communicate, and act.

It is not a pure-proof route and it is not a narrow controls curriculum.

It is a dependency-aware path through the parts of the site that support:

- dynamical systems
- sensing and signal pipelines
- feedback and optimal control
- stochastic decision-making
- computation and simulation

## Who This Is For

Use this roadmap if your goal is any of the following:

- understand state-space models, stability, and feedback
- read signal, sensing, or communication papers without treating the math as black boxes
- connect numerical methods to simulation, inversion, and large systems
- move toward robotics, estimation, scientific computing, or sequential decision-making

If your goal is mostly statistical learning theory or proof-heavy ML theory, [AI / ML Theory](ai-ml-theory.qmd) is the cleaner roadmap.

## Main Sequence

Use this as the default order.

1. [Algebra Repair](../topics/foundation/algebra-repair/index.qmd)
2. [Linear Algebra](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd)
3. [Single-Variable Calculus](../topics/foundation/single-variable-calculus/index.qmd)
4. [Multivariable Calculus](../topics/foundation/multivariable-calculus/index.qmd)
5. [Probability](../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd)
6. [Statistics](../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd)
7. [ODEs and Dynamical Systems](../topics/advanced/odes-and-dynamical-systems/index.qmd)
8. [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd)
9. [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd)
10. [Signal Processing and Estimation](../topics/advanced/signal-processing-and-estimation/index.qmd)
11. [Control and Dynamics](../topics/advanced/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd)
12. [Stochastic Control and Dynamic Programming](../topics/advanced/stochastic-control-and-dynamic-programming/index.qmd)
13. [Information Theory](../topics/advanced/information-theory/index.qmd)

That sequence is now largely live on the site end to end, including complete first-pass modules for [ODEs and Dynamical Systems](../topics/advanced/odes-and-dynamical-systems/index.qmd), [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd), [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd), [Signal Processing and Estimation](../topics/advanced/signal-processing-and-estimation/index.qmd), [Control and Dynamics](../topics/advanced/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd), [Stochastic Control and Dynamic Programming](../topics/advanced/stochastic-control-and-dynamic-programming/index.qmd), and [Information Theory](../topics/advanced/information-theory/index.qmd).

## Why This Order Works

### Linear Algebra And Calculus First

Engineering systems keep reusing the same objects:

- vectors and matrices
- derivatives and local approximation
- gradients, Jacobians, and linearization
- quadratic forms and spectral structure

Without that language, state-space models, filters, and operators become notation to memorize instead of reusable tools.

### Dynamics Before Feedback

Feedback only makes sense once a reader can describe how a system evolves on its own.

That is why [ODEs and Dynamical Systems](../topics/advanced/odes-and-dynamical-systems/index.qmd) comes before [Control and Dynamics](../topics/advanced/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd).

First learn:

- state variables
- solution curves
- equilibria
- local stability

Then it becomes natural to ask how inputs and feedback change that behavior.

### Numerical Methods Before Large-Scale Systems Work

Real systems work is rarely only symbolic.

It depends on:

- solving linear systems
- computing eigenstructure
- simulating trajectories
- handling least squares and regularization
- controlling numerical error

That is why [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd) belongs in the core systems path instead of as an optional afterthought.

### Probability And Statistics Before Estimation Under Uncertainty

Signals, sensors, and controllers rarely operate in perfect conditions.

Noise, hidden state, uncertainty, and limited data force a reader to think probabilistically.

That is why [Probability](../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd) and [Statistics](../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd) come before the deeper estimation and stochastic-control material.

## Shared Core Bridge Into Systems

Before branching, there is one short shared systems core:

1. state-space modeling
2. signals and channels
3. noisy estimation
4. feedback and stability
5. computation and simulation

Useful bridge pages for that shared core are:

- [Applications > Control and Dynamics](../applications/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd)
- [Applications > Signal and Communication](../applications/signal-and-communication/index.qmd)
- [State, Sensing, and Actuation](../applications/control-and-dynamics/state-sensing-and-actuation.qmd)
- [Signals, Channels, and Noisy Measurements](../applications/signal-and-communication/signals-channels-and-noisy-measurements.qmd)
- [Estimation under Noise](../applications/control-and-dynamics/estimation-under-noise.qmd)
- [Sampling, Bandwidth, and Reconstruction in Practice](../applications/signal-and-communication/sampling-bandwidth-and-reconstruction-in-practice.qmd)

## Branch Points

After the shared core, most readers should branch by goal instead of forcing one long linear route.

### Control And Robotics Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- feedback
- stability
- controllability and observability
- trajectory planning
- MPC and constrained control

Best current pages:

- [Control and Dynamics](../topics/advanced/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd)
- [Applications > Control and Dynamics](../applications/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd)

### Sensing And Communication Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- filtering and denoising
- sampling and bandwidth
- decoding and error tradeoffs
- sensing and inverse reconstruction
- information limits

Best current pages:

- [Signal Processing and Estimation](../topics/advanced/signal-processing-and-estimation/index.qmd)
- [Information Theory](../topics/advanced/information-theory/index.qmd)
- [Applications > Signal and Communication](../applications/signal-and-communication/index.qmd)

### Scientific Computing Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- stable simulation
- numerical linear algebra
- time-stepping
- inverse problems
- approximation and error control

Best current pages:

- [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd)
- [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd)
- [Inverse Problems, Sensing, and Reconstruction](../applications/signal-and-communication/inverse-problems-sensing-and-reconstruction.qmd)

### Stochastic Decision-Making Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- noisy dynamics
- Bellman methods
- partial observability
- LQG and filtering
- RL/control bridges

Best current pages:

- [Stochastic Control and Dynamic Programming](../topics/advanced/stochastic-control-and-dynamic-programming/index.qmd)
- [Learning, Identification, and RL Bridges](../applications/control-and-dynamics/learning-identification-and-rl-bridges.qmd)

## Pages On The Site That Already Support This Roadmap

### Strongest Current Systems Math Support

- [ODEs and Dynamical Systems](../topics/advanced/odes-and-dynamical-systems/index.qmd)
- [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd)
- [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd)
- [Signal Processing and Estimation](../topics/advanced/signal-processing-and-estimation/index.qmd)
- [Control and Dynamics](../topics/advanced/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd)
- [Stochastic Control and Dynamic Programming](../topics/advanced/stochastic-control-and-dynamic-programming/index.qmd)
- [Information Theory](../topics/advanced/information-theory/index.qmd)

### Strongest Current Systems Bridge Pages

- [Applications > Control and Dynamics](../applications/control-and-dynamics/index.qmd)
- [Applications > Signal and Communication](../applications/signal-and-communication/index.qmd)
- [Optimal Control and Trajectory Planning](../applications/control-and-dynamics/optimal-control-and-trajectory-planning.qmd)
- [Constraints, MPC, and Safe Operation](../applications/control-and-dynamics/constraints-mpc-and-safe-operation.qmd)
- [Detection, Decoding, and Error Tradeoffs](../applications/signal-and-communication/detection-decoding-and-error-tradeoffs.qmd)
- [Modern Bridges: Representation Learning, Sensing, and Communication](../applications/signal-and-communication/modern-bridges-representation-learning-sensing-and-communication.qmd)

## Paper Reading Overlay

Do not wait until the whole roadmap is complete before touching systems papers.

Run a light reading overlay in parallel:

1. [How to Read a Paper](../paper-lab/how-to-read-a-paper.qmd)
2. one application-facing systems bridge page
3. one matching advanced module page

A good current sequence is:

1. [Signals, Channels, and Noisy Measurements](../applications/signal-and-communication/signals-channels-and-noisy-measurements.qmd)
2. [Signals, Convolution, and Linear Time-Invariant Systems](../topics/advanced/signal-processing-and-estimation/concepts/signals-convolution-and-linear-time-invariant-systems.qmd)
3. [Detection, Decoding, and Error Tradeoffs](../applications/signal-and-communication/detection-decoding-and-error-tradeoffs.qmd)
4. [Channel Coding, Capacity, and Converse Proofs](../topics/advanced/information-theory/concepts/channel-coding-capacity-and-converse-proofs.qmd)

## Common Next Directions

After the current route, the strongest adjacent additions would be:

- `Stochastic Processes`, for Brownian motion, SDEs, MCMC, and diffusion-side probability
- `Applications > Scientific Computing`, for simulation and PDE-facing bridges
- `Applications > Optimization and Inference`, for inverse problems, variational methods, and estimator-design workflows

## Sources and Further Reading

- [18.03 Differential Equations syllabus](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-03-differential-equations-spring-2010/pages/syllabus/) - `First pass` - official MIT anchor for the continuous-time modeling side of systems thinking. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [6.003 Signals and Systems lecture notes](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/6-003-signals-and-systems-fall-2011/pages/lecture-notes/) - `First pass` - official MIT signal-and-system anchor for channels, convolution, and acquisition language. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [6.241J lecture notes](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/6-241j-dynamic-systems-and-control-spring-2011/pages/lecture-notes/) - `First pass` - official MIT state-space and control anchor. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [16.323 lecture notes](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/16-323-principles-of-optimal-control-spring-2008/pages/lecture-notes/) - `Second pass` - official MIT optimal-control anchor once planning and stochastic control begin to matter. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [EE278 / Introduction to Statistical Signal Processing](https://web.stanford.edu/class/ee278/) - `Second pass` - official Stanford estimation anchor for noisy sensing and hidden-state inference. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [EE363 bulletin](https://bulletin.stanford.edu/courses/2273941) - `Second pass` - official Stanford control anchor for linear systems and feedback language. Checked `2026-04-25`.
- [AA228 / Decision Making Under Uncertainty](https://aa228.stanford.edu/) - `Paper bridge` - strong Stanford bridge once stochastic control, planning, and partial observability begin to overlap. Checked `2026-04-25`.
